title: RRI
text: RedoxRRI formalises holobiont resilience coordinated redox buffering across plant, soil, microbial compartments. Rather analysing domain independently, framework models resilience emergent, cross-domain property can expressed either scalar index (RRI) compositional allocation simplex space. RedoxRRI provides transparent extensible framework quantify holobiont redox resilience integrating three complementary domains: Plant physiology (oxidative / nitrosative buffering stress traits) Soil redox stability (Eh redox-coupled chemistry) Microbial resilience Microbial resilience, represented single blended domain can incorporate: microbial abundance functional composition (micro_data) microbial network organization (igraph network metrics) primary output package : per-sample Redox Resilience Index (RRI) scaled [0,1]) ternary-ready compositional representation (Physio, Soil, Micro) row sums equal 1 optional system-level index stored attr(x, “RRI_index”) simulate_redox_holobiont() can create rich synthetic benchmark dataset plant, soil, microbial, trait, optional gene-level layers. richness useful demonstration, testing, method development, users need measure every variable generated simulator. RedoxRRI designed work : simulated benchmark data generated inside package, user-supplied external datasets field, greenhouse, mesocosm, laboratory experiments. key requirement every simulated variable present. key requirement input tables row-aligned, meaning row domain table refers biological sample row id. minimum, users provide: id: sample metadata, including time grouping variables needed; ROS_flux: one plant physiological variables; Eh_stability: one soil redox soil chemistry variables; micro_data: one microbial abundance, functional, trait, molecular variables.

title: RRI
text: domain (Physiology, Soil, Microbial) first summarized one-dimensional latent score per sample using user-selected method (e.g., PCA, FA, UMAP, WGCNA). latent scores scaled range [0, 1]. Redox Resilience Index (RRI) computed weighted linear combination scaled domain scores. domain weights user-defined must sum one. Higher RRI values indicate samples stronger physiological buffering, greater soil redox stability, higher microbial resilience relative samples dataset. Optional coupling terms can included capture coherence among domains, allowing index reflect domain magnitudes also agreement joint stability.

what: Microbial domain as a blended score
title: RRI
text: preserve three-part (Physiology-Soil-Microbial) ternary representation, microbial resilience modeled single blended domain score. microbial abundance (functional composition) data microbial network data available, microbial domain score computed weighted blend two components. parameter alpha_micro controls relative contribution: alpha_micro = 1: abundance functional composition alpha_micro = 0: network structure alpha_micro = 0.5: equal contribution (default) design allows flexible emphasis microbial composition, microbial function, . Functional information can include gene abundance, functional gene coverage, metagenomic features, metatranscriptomic expression, gene upregulation summaries. inputs integrated single microbial resilience domain maintaining consistent three-domain representation suitable compositional ternary visualization. example, users may include variables : taxonomic abundance features, ASVs, OTUs, MAGs, taxa; microbial trait scores; functional gene abundance, narG, nirK, nosZ, mtrA, dsrA, mcrA; metatranscriptomic expression values; log2 fold-change upregulation scores; pathway-level guild-level summaries; redox process indicators, denitrification, sulfate reduction, methanogenesis, extracellular electron transfer, ROS detoxification scores. features can combined one microbial feature table running rri_pipeline_st():

y, use = "complete.obs", method = method) }  n_obs <- nrow(df_val) cor_val <- cor_complete(df_val$latent, df_val$rri) lm_fit <- stats::lm(rri ~ latent, data = df_val) r_sq <- summary(lm_fit)$r.squared  stat_label <- paste0(   "r = ", round(cor_val, 2),   ", R² = ", round(r_sq, 2),   ", n = ", n_obs )  p2A <- ggplot2::ggplot(df_val, ggplot2::aes(x = latent, y = rri)) +   ggplot2::geom_point(alpha = 0.25, size = 1.1) +   ggplot2::geom_smooth(     method = "lm",     se = TRUE,     color = "#B2182B",     linewidth = 0.9   ) +   ggplot2::annotate(     "text",     x = min(df_val$latent, na.rm = TRUE),     y = max(df_val$rri, na.rm = TRUE),     hjust = 0,     vjust = 1,     size = 3.2,     label = stat_label   ) +   ggplot2::labs(     x = "True latent redox state",     y = "Redox Resilience Index (RRI)"   ) +   theme_ems()  df_ts <- df_val |>   dplyr::group_by(time) |>   dplyr::summarise(     latent_mean = mean(latent, na.rm = TRUE),     rri_mean = mean(rri, na.rm = TRUE),     rri_se = stats::sd(rri, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(dplyr::n()),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   dplyr::mutate(     rri_lo = rri_mean - 1.96 * rri_se,     rri_hi = rri_mean + 1.96 * rri_se   )  p2B <- ggplot2::ggplot(df_ts, ggplot2::aes(x = time)) +   ggplot2::geom_line(     ggplot2::aes(y = latent_mean),     color = "grey35",     linewidth = 1   ) +   ggplot2::geom_ribbon(     ggplot2::aes(ymin = rri_lo, ymax = rri_hi),     fill = "#2C7BB6",     alpha = 0.18   ) +   ggplot2::geom_line(     ggplot2::aes(y = rri_mean),     color = "#2C7BB6",     linewidth = 1.2   ) +   ggplot2::labs(     x = "Time",     y = "Scaled value (0–1)"   ) +   theme_ems()  p2 <- p2A + p2B +   patchwork::plot_annotation(     title = "Validation of latent recovery and temporal tracking",     tag_levels = "A"   ) p2 title: RRI
text: understand cross-domain coupling contributes holobiont stability, simulate systems increasing levels domain decoupling quantify three structural properties resulting RRI decomposition: Mean pairwise correlation among domains Net covariance contribution aggregate variance Cross-domain coordination strength Higher decoupling reduce coordination among domains therefore expect metrics decline increasing decoupling.
code: library(patchwork) library(dplyr) library(tidyr) ## Warning: package 'tidyr' was built under R version 4.5.2 library(tibble) library(purrr)  # decoupling gradient dec_grid <- seq(0.1, 0.8, by = 0.1)  # number of stochastic replicates n_rep <- 50   compute_structure <- function(decoupling, rep){    sim <- simulate_redox_holobiont(     seed = 1000 + rep + round(decoupling * 100),     decoupling = decoupling   )    res <- rri_pipeline_st(     ROS_flux = sim$ROS_flux,     Eh_stability = sim$Eh_stability,     micro_data = sim$micro_data,     reducer = "mfa",     scaling = "pnorm",     comp_space = "clr",     add_compensation = TRUE,     compensation_weight = 0.2   )    domains <- res$row_scores[, c("Physio","Soil","Micro")]    # standardize domains   domains <- scale(domains)    # Panel A — synchrony   cor_mat <- cor(domains)   mean_corr <- mean(cor_mat[upper.tri(cor_mat)])    # Panel B — domain divergence   domain_divergence <- mean(apply(domains, 1, sd))    # Panel C — system instability   rri_instability <- mean(abs(diff(res$row_scores$RRI)), na.rm = TRUE)    tibble(     decoupling = decoupling,     mean_corr = mean_corr,     domain_divergence = domain_divergence,     rri_instability = rri_instability   ) }  # run simulations df <- tidyr::expand_grid(   decoupling = dec_grid,   rep = 1:n_rep ) |>   pmap_dfr(~compute_structure(..1, ..2))    # rename to avoid summarise masking bug df <- df |> rename(mean_corr_raw = mean_corr)    # summarise statistics df_summary <- df |>   group_by(decoupling) |>   summarise(      mean_corr = mean(mean_corr_raw),     corr_se = sd(mean_corr_raw) / sqrt(n()),      div_mean = mean(domain_divergence),     div_se = sd(domain_divergence) / sqrt(n()),      instab_mean = mean(rri_instability),     instab_se = sd(rri_instability) / sqrt(n()),      .groups = "drop"   )    # Panel A — domain synchrony pA <- ggplot(df_summary, aes(decoupling, mean_corr)) +    geom_ribbon(     aes(ymin = mean_corr - corr_se,         ymax = mean_corr + corr_se),     fill = "#3b78a8",     alpha = 0.25   ) +    geom_line(color = "#3b78a8", linewidth = 1) +   geom_point(color = "#3b78a8", size = 2) +    labs(     x = "Simulated cross-domain decoupling",     y = "Mean pairwise domain correlation"   ) +    theme_classic()    # Panel B — domain divergence pB <- ggplot(df_summary, aes(decoupling, div_mean)) +    geom_ribbon(     aes(ymin = div_mean - div_se,         ymax = div_mean + div_se),     fill = "#d55e00",     alpha = 0.25   ) +    geom_line(color = "#d55e00", linewidth = 1) +   geom_point(color = "#d55e00", size = 2) +    labs(     x = "Simulated cross-domain decoupling",     y = "Cross-domain divergence"   ) +    theme_classic()    # Panel C — system instability pC <- ggplot(df_summary, aes(decoupling, instab_mean)) +    geom_ribbon(     aes(ymin = instab_mean - instab_se,         ymax = instab_mean + instab_se),     fill = "#7570b3",     alpha = 0.25   ) +    geom_line(color = "#7570b3", linewidth = 1) +   geom_point(color = "#7570b3", size = 2) +    labs(     x = "Simulated cross-domain decoupling",     y = "RRI fluctuation magnitude (system instability)"   ) +    theme_classic()    # combine panels fig4 <- (pA | pB | pC) +   plot_annotation(     title = "Structural consequences of cross-domain decoupling",     subtitle = "Increasing decoupling reduces synchrony while increasing divergence and systemic instability",     tag_levels = "A"   )  fig4

title: RRI

title: RRI
text: RRI R package computing, visualizing, interpreting Holobiont Redox Resilience Index (RRI). framework treats resilience emergent property coordinated redox buffering across three interacting domains: Physio: plant physiological buffering stress response. Soil: soil redox chemistry electron-acceptor stability. Micro: microbial abundance, function, traits, network resilience. package supports spatial structure, temporal dynamics, directionally identified latent scores, ternary visualization, sensitivity analysis, perturbation-recovery metrics.

title: RRI
text: Integrates plant, soil, microbial redox-resilience indicators. Computes per-sample RRI scores scaled [0, 1]. Produces ternary-ready Physio-Soil-Micro compositional scores. Supports PCA, factor analysis, NMF, UMAP, WGCNA, MFA-style workflows. Uses direction anchoring higher scores consistently indicate stronger resilience. Supports microbial abundance, functional genes, MetaT expression, upregulation summaries, traits, optional networks. Computes perturbation-recovery metrics including amplitude, lag, overshoot, incomplete recovery, recovery rate, hysteresis, trajectory class. Provides diagnostic sensitivity utilities validation robustness checks.

title: RRI
text: Redox resilience holobiont scale emerges interacting biological subsystems rather single measurable trait. RRI summarizes domain using latent score derived multivariate indicators. domain scores directionally aligned, scaled, integrated composite index. three core domains : Physio: plant oxidative buffering physiological stress response. Soil: soil redox chemistry, hydrology, electron-transfer stability. Micro: microbial abundance, functional capacity, traits, genes, expression, network organization. microbial domain flexible. may contain taxonomic abundance, functional composition, microbial traits, gene abundance, functional gene coverage, metagenomic features, metatranscriptomic expression, gene upregulation summaries, pathway-level features, ecological network metrics.

title: RRI
text: install vignettes: Basic example Adding microbial functional information  Users limited microbial abundance data. Functional microbial information can added microbial domain available. minimal expanded workflows valid. expanded workflow simply uses richer definition microbial resilience. Ternary visualization  Perturbation-recovery metrics RRI trajectories can summarized using direction-aware perturbation-recovery metrics. Recovery metrics include perturbation amplitude, lag time, overshoot, incomplete recovery, recovery rate, recovery fit diagnostics, recovery timescale, half-recovery time, hysteresis, trajectory class. Output structure primary output rri_pipeline_st() contains: row_scores: absolute Physio, Soil, Micro, RRI scores. row_scores_comp: compositional Physio-Soil-Micro allocation scores. meta: model settings system-level RRI metadata. compositional output directly compatible plot_RRI_ternary(). Additional utilities RRI includes helper functions diagnostics, sensitivity analysis, recovery metrics, visualization:
code: install.packages("remotes")  remotes::install_github("mghotbi/RRI") install.packages("remotes") install.packages("BiocManager")  BiocManager::install(c("BiocStyle", "rmarkdown", "knitr"))  remotes::install_github(   "mghotbi/RRI",   build_vignettes = TRUE,   dependencies = TRUE ) library(RRI)  sim <- simulate_redox_holobiont(seed = 1)  res <- rri_pipeline_st(   ROS_flux = sim$ROS_flux,   Eh_stability = sim$Eh_stability,   micro_data = sim$micro_data,   id = sim$id,   reducer = "per_domain",   scaling = "pnorm",   direction_phys = "auto",   direction_anchor_phys = "FvFm",   direction_soil = "auto",   direction_anchor_soil = "Eh",   direction_micro = "higher_is_better" )  head(res$row_scores) head(res$row_scores_comp) micro_features <- sim$micro_data  if (!is.null(sim$micro_traits)) {   micro_features <- cbind(micro_features, sim$micro_traits) }  if (!is.null(sim$gene_abundance)) {   micro_features <- cbind(micro_features, sim$gene_abundance) }  if (!is.null(sim$gene_log2fc)) {   micro_features <- cbind(micro_features, sim$gene_log2fc) }  res_full <- rri_pipeline_st(   ROS_flux = sim$ROS_flux,   Eh_stability = sim$Eh_stability,   micro_data = micro_features,   id = sim$id,   reducer = "per_domain",   scaling = "pnorm",   direction_phys = "auto",   direction_anchor_phys = "FvFm",   direction_soil = "auto",   direction_anchor_soil = "Eh",   direction_micro = "higher_is_better" ) plot_RRI_ternary(res$row_scores_comp) rec <- rri_recovery_metrics(   res = res,   id = sim$id,   time_col = "time",   group_cols = c("plot", "depth", "plant_id"),   perturb_start = 5,   perturb_end = 7 )  head(rec)  rri_metric_table() rri_latent_correlation(res, sim$latent_truth) rri_compensation_index(res) rri_sensitivity(res)  plot_RRI_ternary(res$row_scores_comp) plot_rri_recovery_map(rec) plot_rri_recovery_landscape(rec)

what: Brief theory: redox resilience as buffering, connectivity, memory, and recovery
title: RRI
text: Redox resilience reflects capacity soil-plant-microbiome system absorb, transmit, recover perturbations alter electron flow. framework, resilience treated single trait, emergent property several interacting redox processes: Capacity: size electron-accepting electron-donating pools, Fe/Mn oxides, organic matter, clays, humic substances, redox-active phases. High capacity allows system buffer electron imbalance crossing critical redox thresholds. Connectivity: physical biochemical linkage among electron donors, electron acceptors, roots, microbes, minerals, water-filled pore networks. Connectivity controls whether electrons, oxygen, substrates, microbial processes remain spatially coupled become locally isolated. Kinetics: rate redox reactions proceed relative hydrological environmental forcing. Fast forcing combined slow microbial, mineral, diffusion-mediated responses can generate lag, transient disequilibrium, redox pulses. Memory: persistence altered mineral, organic, microbial, plant states original perturbation ends. Memory can arise residual reduced minerals, redox-active organic matter, shifted microbial communities, enzyme pools, plant acclimation. Hysteresis: path dependence wetting drying, reduction oxidation, disturbance recovery follow trajectory. Hysteresis indicates current state depends present conditions, also sequence previous states. Recovery: trajectory perturbation, including return original state, transient overshoot, incomplete recovery, transition new equilibrium. RRI summarizes interacting processes integrating plant physiological buffering, soil redox stability, microbial resilience single directionally aligned index. Perturbation-recovery functions quantify RRI trajectory changes time, including amplitude, lag, overshoot, incomplete recovery, recovery rate, hysteresis, trajectory class. Citation Ghotbi, M. et al. RRI: framework holobiont-level redox resilience (manuscript preparation). Quantify perturbation-recovery metrics. Generate ternary recovery-metric visualizations. Inspect formal recovery-metric dictionary. simulated dataset contains plant physiological variables, rhizosphere oxygen-flux proxies, soil redox chemistry, hydrological variables, dissolved organic carbon, microbial abundance features, microbial redox trait proxies, optional functional gene abundance MetaT-style expression features. generated data fully synthetic provided examples, testing, benchmarking, teaching, method development. calibrated specific ecosystem. Users may replace simulated inputs external datasets, provided rows aligned across id, ROS_flux, Eh_stability, micro_data.

title: Cross-Domain Compensation Index — rri_compensation_index
text: Computes scale-invariant cross-domain compensation index based mean pairwise correlations among Physio, Soil, Micro domain scores.

title: Cross-Domain Compensation Index — rri_compensation_index
code: rri_compensation_index(res)

title: Cross-Domain Compensation Index — rri_compensation_index
text: res object returned rri_pipeline_st().

title: Cross-Domain Compensation Index — rri_compensation_index
text: single numeric compensation index \([-1, 1]\). Positive values indicate compensatory dynamics (negative cross-domain correlations), negative values indicate synchrony.

title: Cross-Domain Compensation Index — rri_compensation_index
text: compensation index defined : $$   \mathrm{Comp} = -\frac{2}{k(k-1)} \sum_{<j} \mathrm{Cor}(D_i, D_j) $$ \(k = 3\) \(D_i\) domain-level scores. Using correlations rather covariances ensures scale invariance boundedness across datasets.

title: Cross-Domain Compensation Index — rri_compensation_index
code: # ---- Simulate small holobiont dataset ---- sim <- simulate_redox_holobiont(   n_plot = 2,   n_depth = 1,   n_plant = 2,   n_time = 8,   p_micro = 20,   seed = 1 )  # ---- Compute RedoxRRI ---- res <- rri_pipeline_st(   ROS_flux = sim$ROS_flux,   Eh_stability = sim$Eh_stability,   micro_data = sim$micro_data,   id = sim$id,   reducer = "per_domain",   scaling = "pnorm" )  # ---- Compute cross-domain compensation index ---- comp <- rri_compensation_index(res)

title: Holobiont Redox Resilience Index (RRI) with Spatio-Temporal Dynamics — rri_pipeline_st
text: Computes holobiont-level Redox Resilience Index (RRI) integrating plant physiological traits, soil redox chemistry, microbial resilience unified, directionally identifiable index. framework supports static (snapshot), rolling-window, event-based resilience modes, optional compositional geometry (clr), multiblock reduction via MFA, covariance-based compensation.

NULL, rows treated one trajectory. state_col Character scalar. Column res$row_scores used system state. Defaults "RRI". perturb_start Numeric scalar. Perturbation onset time, \(t_0\). perturb_end Numeric scalar. Perturbation termination time, \(t_1\). detect_threshold Numeric scalar. Absolute deviation baseline required define detectable response onset. equilibrium_window Integer scalar. title: Quantify perturbation-recovery metrics from an RRI trajectory — rri_recovery_metrics
text: data frame one row per trajectory. Columns include: x0 Estimated pre-perturbation baseline state. xmin_perturb Minimum state perturbation. xmax_perturb Maximum state perturbation. x_extreme Perturbation state farthest baseline. perturb_direction Perturbation direction: "increase", "decrease", "neutral". xmin_recovery Minimum state recovery. xmax_recovery Maximum state recovery. xeq Estimated post-recovery equilibrium. Direction-aware perturbation amplitude. A_norm Amplitude normalized abs(x0). tau_lag Detectable response lag. O Direction-aware overshoot. O_norm Overshoot normalized abs(x0). Incomplete recovery. I_norm Incomplete recovery normalized abs(x0). k Estimated exponential recovery rate constant. k_r2 Coefficient determination log-linear recovery fit. k_n Number recovery observations used estimate k. k_flag Diagnostic flag recovery-rate estimation. tau_r Characteristic recovery time, \(1/k\). t_half Half-recovery time, \(\log(2)/k\). H Hysteresis estimate. trajectory_class Classified recovery trajectory.

title: Quantify perturbation-recovery metrics from an RRI trajectory — rri_recovery_metrics
text: function interprets RRI trajectory perturbation-response state: $$x(t)$$ \(x(t)\) observed redox-resilience state holobiont time \(t\). RedoxRRI applications, \(x(t)\) usually corresponds per-sample RRI score, continuous state column res$row_scores can supplied state_col. trajectory partitioned three temporal domains: baseline: \(t < t_0\); perturbation: \(t_0 \le t \le t_1\); recovery: \(t > t_1\). Baseline state estimated : $$ x_0 = \frac{1}{n_0} \sum_{t_i < t_0} x(t_i) $$ Perturbation amplitude direction-aware defined largest absolute displacement baseline perturbation: $$ = \max_{t_0 \le t_i \le t_1} |x(t_i) - x_0| $$ perturbation extremum : $$ x_{\mathrm{extreme}} = x(t^*), \quad t^* = \arg\max_{t_0 \le t_i \le t_1} |x(t_i) - x_0| $$ perturbation direction : $$ d = \mathrm{sign}(x_{\mathrm{extreme}} - x_0) $$ Detectable response lag : $$ \tau_{\mathrm{lag}} = t_{\mathrm{detect}} - t_0 $$ \(t_{\mathrm{detect}}\) first time satisfying: $$ |x(t) - x_0| > \epsilon $$ \(\epsilon\) specified detect_threshold. Overshoot direction-aware. perturbation decreases state, overshoot recovery baseline. perturbation increases state, overshoot recovery baseline: $$ O = \left\{ \begin{array}{ll} \max(0, x_{\max,\mathrm{rec}} - x_0), & d < 0 \\ \max(0, x_0 - x_{\min,\mathrm{rec}}), & d > 0 \\ 0, & d = 0 \end{array} \right. $$ Post-recovery equilibrium estimated final recovery observations: $$ x_{\mathrm{eq}} = \frac{1}{m} \sum_{j=1}^{m} x_j $$ \(m\) determined equilibrium_window. Incomplete recovery : $$ = |x_{\mathrm{eq}} - x_0| $$ Recovery kinetics estimated log-linearized exponential relaxation model: $$ x(t) = x_{\mathrm{eq}} + [x(t_1) - x_{\mathrm{eq}}] \exp[-k(t - t_1)] $$ equivalently: $$ \log |x(t) - x_{\mathrm{eq}}| = - k(t - t_1) $$ characteristic recovery timescale : $$ \tau_r = \frac{1}{k} $$ half-recovery time : $$ t_{1/2} = \frac{\log(2)}{k} $$ forcing_col supplied, hysteresis approximated : $$ H \approx \frac{1}{n} \sum_i |x_{\mathrm{rec}}(F_i) - x_{\mathrm{pert}}(F_i)| $$ recovery perturbation states compared matched forcing values. Normalized metrics returned : $$ A_n = / |x_0|, \quad O_n = O / |x_0|, \quad I_n = / |x_0| $$ Trajectories classified using ordered decision rules. Incomplete recovery priority hysteresis, hysteresis overshoot, overshoot simple fast slow recovery. Default thresholds operational calibrated specific experimental systems sufficient empirical replication available.

Use NULL stochastic runs. include_graph Logical. TRUE igraph available, returns simulated microbial network. depth_labels Optional character vector length n_depth. Controls whether synthetic functional gene coverage, MetaT expression, , neither returned. disturbance_strength Numeric [0, 1]. Magnitude disturbance pulse. disturbance_center Optional numeric. NULL, disturbance centred median time point. disturbance_width Numeric > 0. Pulse width fraction season length. seasonal_amp Numeric >= 0. Amplitude seasonal forcing. seasonal_phase Numeric. Phase shift seasonal forcing radians. stochastic_reassembly Logical. TRUE, microbial counts include stochastic reassembly component. decoupling Numeric [0, 1]. Cross-domain decoupling. Higher values weaken deterministic coupling latent redox state observed domains. zero_inflation Numeric [0, 1]. Probability microbial abundance entry set zero. MNAR_strength Numeric [0, 1]. Strength missing---random Eh dropout strongly reduced states. Eh_dropout_threshold Numeric [0, 1]. Latent redox threshold Eh dropout can occur. micro_mean Numeric > 0. Baseline mean intensity microbial counts. micro_slope Numeric >= 0. Coupling strength reduced redox state microbial abundance. micro_lambda_min Numeric > 0. title: Simulate a holobiont redox dataset with spatio-temporal structure — simulate_redox_holobiont
text: named list : id: Experimental design data frame. ROS_flux: Plant physiological rhizosphere boundary-flux variables. Eh_stability: Soil redox, hydrological, carbon, electron-buffering variables. micro_data: Microbial abundance feature matrix. micro_traits: Microbial redox functional trait proxies. gene_abundance: Optional synthetic functional gene coverage/abundance matrix. gene_expression: Optional synthetic MetaT expression matrix. gene_log2fc: Optional synthetic MetaT log2 fold-change matrix relative pre-disturbance baseline. latent_truth: Underlying latent redox state [0, 1]. graph: Optional igraph object.

title: Simulate a holobiont redox dataset with spatio-temporal structure — simulate_redox_holobiont
text: simulator designed demonstrations, benchmarking, package examples, method development. represents simplified soil-plant-microbiome continuum hydrological forcing modifies latent redox state, soil water content, electron-donor supply, electron-acceptor availability, plant stress, radial oxygen loss, rhizodeposition, microbial abundance, redox process potential, functional gene response. microbial functional layer includes synthetic proxies aerobic respiration, nitrate reduction, Fe/Mn/humic extracellular electron transfer, sulfate reduction, methanogenesis, redox mediator production, ROS detoxification, AMF connectivity, protistan grazing, viral lysis, microbial memory, redox flexibility. generated data synthetic intended represent real ecosystem. Parameter values chosen generate biologically interpretable covariance structure rather calibrated field realism. #' Users required measure every variable generated simulator. simulator returns rich synthetic benchmark dataset, external datasets may contain fewer variables. Downstream RedoxRRI functions require row-aligned data frames domains used analysis. example, rri_pipeline_st() can run small set plant, soil, microbial variables, rri_recovery_metrics() can run directly precomputed RRI trajectory stored res$row_scores.
